Daniel needed to own himself emotionally so he could rediscover what was truly possible so he could set up a strategy and revive the deal he originally wanted. Tony guided him through five steps for retrieving a deal. By setting up a positive identity for his adversary, he found a way to influence his rogue partner. By focusing on value creation, he can rekindle the other partners interests and curiosity in what Daniel can do for the deal. By creating a new value proposition, he'll be able to put a new deal on the table that will truly represent the value he can bring. He'll reset his options so he's thinking creatively and developing a solid walk away plan if she'd need that. And he'll plan the conversation so that he knows what to do when his adversary responds.
